root_text.sn47.28:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn47.28:1.3: “katamesaṁ, āvuso anuruddha, dhammānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā mahābhiññataṁ patto”ti?
root_text.sn47.28:1.4: “Catunnaṁ, āvuso, satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā mahābhiññataṁ patto.
root_text.sn47.28:2.2: Idhāhaṁ, āvuso, kāye kāyānupassī viharāmi ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.28:2.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharāmi ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.28:2.6: Imesaṁ khvāhaṁ, āvuso, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā mahābhiññataṁ patto.
root_text.sn47.28:2.7: Imesañca panāhaṁ, āvuso, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā sahassaṁ lokaṁ abhijānāmī”ti.

translation_text.sn47.28:1.3: “Reverend Anuruddha, what things have you developed and cultivated to attain great direct knowledge?”
translation_text.sn47.28:1.4: “Reverend, I attained great direct knowledge by developing and cultivating the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.28:2.2: It’s when I meditate observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.28:2.3: I meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.28:2.5: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.28:2.6: I attained great direct knowledge by developing and cultivating these four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.28:2.7: And it’s because of developing and cultivating these four kinds of mindfulness meditation that I directly know the thousandfold galaxy.”

variant_text.sn47.28:1.3: katamesaṁ, āvuso anuruddha → āyasmā anuruddho (bj, sya-all)
